priorities.check_method: S61 P1 (SEVER_BOND orchestrator extract) verified. Moved the 43-LOC inline dispatch SEVER_BOND body to NEW src/state/severBond.ts (applySeverBond) -- dispatch() now uniformly 1-line delegations (mirrors godlyActions/sparkLifecycle/placeFromFree). world.ts 488->445 LOC. CYCLE-FREE by design: severBond imports World/GameAction TYPE-ONLY (same shape as disruptionManager.ts:43) so no world<->severBond runtime cycle (tsc -b clean confirms). Council Option B: the lone world.ts runtime symbol requirePlayer (throws on missing player) replaced by an inline guarded lookup world.players.get(action.playerId) -- PROVABLY EQUIVALENT since computeBaseCharge returns >0 ONLY for cause=player + existing player + hostile, so the actor is guaranteed present whenever chargeToConsume>0 (requirePlayer throw was unreachable). priorities.check_method: S61 P2 (reducer->render import-boundary guard) verified. NEW src/state/reducerRenderBoundary.test.ts: a transitive RUNTIME import-graph walker rooted at world.ts asserts src/render/ is unreachable -- locks the determinism boundary (a render import drags in Pixi/DOM/GPU side effects, breaking save.replay + host/client sync). HARDENED per S61 Council (both Grok+Gemini flagged the naive check insufficient): follows static imports + value re-exports + dynamic import() + side-effect imports, IGNORES import type/export type (erased at runtime), NO fragile filename allowlist -- godlyOrchestration.ts (the documented state->render ticker-glue seam) is structurally OUT of scope because it CALLS dispatch (imports world.ts:33) rather than being imported by it, so it never enters world.ts import closure (confirmed by reading its 4 runtime render imports + 1 type-only + dispatch-from-world). DETECTOR TEETH (addresses the Council false-confidence concern): +3 self-verification tests -- (1) parser extracts runtime specifiers + skips type-only/bare/mixed, (2) isUnderRender classifier sanity, (3) POSITIVE CONTROL: pointing the walker at godlyOrchestration.ts DOES report render coupling (proves the clean main result is real signal not a parser silently finding nothing). EMPIRICAL: tsc -b exit 0; 4 guard tests green; FULL unit 930/930 (was 926, +4); boundary CLEAN; zero production change (behavior-preserving trivially). CHECK: the impl + teeth tests EMPIRICALLY satisfy the Council hardening mandate (transitive+dynamic+no-allowlist+has-teeth) = the design Council IS the external review. checkpoint e6690b0.

priorities.check_method: S61 P3 (World state-shape type-split) verified. Moved the World interface + GameState/GameMode out of world.ts into NEW src/state/worldTypes.ts (pure-type module); world.ts retains the dispatch seam + makeWorld/dispatch/requirePlayer + GameAction, and re-exports the 3 types via a type-only facade (export type {...} from worldTypes, Grok-recommended direct form) so all ~20 consumers import World from ./world.ts UNCHANGED. world.ts 445->286 LOC (toward §XV 280 cap); worldTypes.ts 175. Cut chosen (World+GameState+GameMode, NOT GameAction): World references only leaf domain types so worldTypes->domain only = NO cycle with reducers; moving GameAction would entangle worldTypes with every reducer sub-action type (Grok-analyst (d): not worth it). Done PROGRAMMATICALLY (Node byte-exact block extraction via markers + sanity asserts, not hand-transcribed) to eliminate transcription risk on the 150-line move. PURE TYPE relocation = provably runtime byte-identical: verbatimModuleSyntax tsc enforces moved surface emits no JS; EMPIRICAL confirm = prod bundle 507.12 KB essentially unchanged vs S60 507.05 (delta is P1 module split; P3 adds 0 runtime bytes) < 550 cap. EMPIRICAL: tsc -b exit 0; FULL unit 930/930 (no test delta, pure type move); npm run build green; e2e 15 pass/1 skip (10 smoke + 5 fog incl structure-ghost memory + SEVER-exercising self-bond Sym C = reducer-core surgery behavior-preserving at RUNTIME). Grok-analyst CHECK PROCEED-WITH-CHANGE, adopted direct re-export; biggest-risk (verbatimModuleSyntax re-export leak) empirically refuted by unchanged bundle. RALPH:PATROL clean. checkpoint 19e383e.

verification.needle: export type { GameMode, GameState, World } from './worldTypes.ts';

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #anti-bloat-orchestrator-extract-typeonly-no-cycle #prime-audit-refute-emitter-myth: extracted world.ts last inline dispatch orchestrator (SEVER_BOND, 43 LOC) into severBond.ts; dispatch() now uniform 1-line delegations. Cycle Grok feared avoided by importing World/GameAction TYPE-ONLY (mirroring disruptionManager) + replacing the lone runtime symbol requirePlayer with an inline guarded lookup that is PROVABLY EQUIVALENT (computeBaseCharge is the existence-guarantor: returns >0 only after a successful world.players.get, so requirePlayer throw was unreachable when chargeToConsume>0). PRIME-AUDIT refuted Grok DEFER via code probe: world.effects is a plain GameEffect[] so push(BOND_SEVERED) is an append NOT a listener call (no re-entrant dispatch), and computeBaseCharge + charge line are adjacent synchronous reads (no interleave). Lesson: when a reviewer asserts a closure/emission/timing hazard in a reducer, verify whether the event is actually a data-array append (no side effect) before deferring; a 1-line non-verbatim change is safe IF you can name the invariant guaranteeing the dropped branch is unreachable AND lock it with a test. +6 charge-consume lock tests cover exactly where save.replay (final-state-only) is blind.

P2 #import-boundary-guard-with-teeth #council-harden-transitive-not-shallow: added a determinism guard walking world.ts transitive RUNTIME import graph asserting src/render/ unreachable. Both Council models independently flagged the FIRST design (shallow file-text-mentions-render + filename allowlist) as false-confidence: misses transitive (state->util->render) + dynamic import() coupling, and a rename breaks the allowlist. HARDENED: full-graph DFS following static+re-export+dynamic+side-effect edges, type-only ignored (erased), NO allowlist -- the one known state->render file (godlyOrchestration) is structurally out of scope (it CALLS dispatch, downstream, not imported by it). KEY LESSON: a guard test that always passes is WORSE than none (false confidence), so every static-analysis guard MUST ship a POSITIVE CONTROL proving it detects the thing it guards against -- here, scanning godlyOrchestration DOES flag render. Otherwise a broken parser returns [] and every assertion goes green. Transferable to any assert-X-never-happens lint/test.

P3 #anti-bloat-type-logic-split-facade #programmatic-block-move-zero-transcription: split world.ts 143-LOC World interface (+GameState/GameMode) into a pure-type worldTypes.ts, keeping the dispatch seam + GameAction in world.ts, with a type-only re-export facade so ~20 consumers stayed unchanged. world.ts 445->286. Two transferable techniques: (1) for a LARGE contiguous block MOVE, do it PROGRAMMATICALLY (Node marker-based byte-exact slice + sanity asserts + write both files) instead of a hand-typed giant Edit old_string -- eliminates transcription error on 150 lines and self-verifies (World-def removed from src, present in dst, re-export added). (2) a type-only relocation is provably runtime-safe under verbatimModuleSyntax (tsc enforces no-emit for type-only import/export), and prod-bundle byte-size is the empirical arbiter that nothing leaked into runtime JS (here unchanged). Grok-analyst tie-broke the boundary: keep GameAction in world.ts (moving it entangles worldTypes with every reducer sub-action type); use the DIRECT export-type-from re-export (canonical verbatimModuleSyntax idiom). Lesson: when an interface references only LEAF domain types it is a clean cut to a types module; when it would pull in sibling-module types, leave it.

analyze_summary: S61 ANALYZE (autonomous §XV batch under a non-autonomous-PDR pivot). DEFINING DECISION (correct scope-discipline): the user-approved PDR (fog-feel tuning) REQUIRES the live user-eye (its own arbiter clause), so executing it autonomously = low-quality guesses, against the highest-quality mandate. PIVOTED to the handoff autonomous-SAFE §XV set (S60 precedent), preserved fog as a LOGGED carry-forward (NOT dropped), ran a real Council (Grok+Gemini) as the absent-user adversarial check. WINS: (1) 3 behavior-preserving priorities shipped + CI-green (P1 severBond 19c1245, P2 import-guard e6690b0, P3 worldTypes 19e383e). (2) PRIME-AUDIT empirically REFUTED Grok P1 DEFER via code probe (world.effects is a data array not an emitter -> no re-entrant dispatch; adjacent synchronous reads -> no interleave) while Gemini independently concurred (empirical-beats-reasoning, same as S60). (3) P2 turned BOTH Councils false-confidence critique into a HARDENED transitive guard WITH a positive control proving teeth. (4) P3 used a PROGRAMMATIC byte-exact block move (zero transcription risk) + prod-bundle size as the no-leak arbiter. world.ts 488->286 LOC across P1+P3 (toward 280 charter); dispatch() now uniform; determinism boundary test-locked. (5) Runtime-verified reducer-core surgery via full e2e (15 pass/1 skip) not just tsc, per the handoff standing warning. EFFICIENCY: GREEN throughout (79K->308K, 31%); upfront Council + Explore-agent survey -> no rework/false-starts. METHOD held under autonomy: every priority empirical-green (tsc+unit+e2e+build) before commit; reflexions 3/3. CARRY: fog-feel tuning + lobbyScreen refactor (both need user) + knip inventory.
